name: Ambedkar: Sonic Imagination Of Casteless Utopia. Book launch with Yogesh Maitreya and Sachin Sreenath
description: Join us for the launch of  Yogesh Maitreya's  book  Ambedkar: Sonic Imagination Of Casteless Utopia ,  in conversation with  Sachin Sreenath . Here are the event details: Date:  25th April, Saturday Time:  6:00 pm Venue:  Champaca, Vasanth Nagar. Click here  to RSVP for the event. About the Book: Ambedkar: Sonic Imagination of Casteless Utopia  by  Yogesh Maitreya  is a powerful, genre-blurring work that reimagines  B. R. Ambedkar  through sound, memory, and radical thought. It traces his journey by looking at the work, time and life of fifteen selected musicians across seven decades, and provides fresh perspectives to not only look at anti-caste movement in Maharashtra, but it also offers fresh perspectives to understand the music of anti-caste movement that shaped Dr Ambedkar into sonic imagination of casteless utopia over the last seven decades. About the Author: Yogesh Maitreya  is a poet, writer and publisher. The author of nine books, he travels across people’s realities and utopias they possess and strive for. In the form of meeting people in Europe and India who are keen to understand Dr Ambedkar, he feels that despite their number being so small, there still exist people in the world who are constantly working in their own ways to make the world a just and beautiful place.  About the Moderator: Sachin Sreenath  is an actor, movement artist and theatre practitioner trained at the Adishakti Theatre Research labs. He's been a theatre practitioner for past 11 years predominantly in Kannada and English spaces. He is also learning Yakshagana, Parai and Carnatic vocals. He is currently pursuing his Masters in Kannada Literature. Limited spots available,  RSVP  at the earliest!

name: Your Stick Will Not Break My Strength: A conversation with Sunil Mohan and Vijeta Kumar
description:
Join us for a conversation with  Vijeta Kumar  and  Sunil Mohan , about his book  Your Stick Will Not Break My Strength.  Here are the event details: Date:  19th April, Sunday Time:  6:00 pm Venue:  Champaca, Vasanth Nagar Click here  to RSVP for the event. About the Book: At its core, the book traces Mohan’s journey as a trans man—from being assigned female at birth to navigating his transition, while constantly questioning what it means to be “male” in a society shaped by rigid ideas of masculinity. It’s not just a personal story, but an exploration of how gender, body, and identity are shaped by social expectations and power structures.  What makes the book especially compelling is that it goes beyond a typical life narrative. Mohan situates his experiences within larger movements—queer, trans, anti-caste, and feminist struggles—reflecting on solidarity, community, silence, and resistance. Rather than positioning himself as a victim, he offers a nuanced, self-aware account of living, questioning, and redefining identity. About the Author: Sunil Mohan  once led the Kerala State Women’s Cricket Team as its captain. He later completed a diploma in electrical engineering and then worked with various NGOs that advocate for the rights of marginalised sexualities and genders. Sunil documented the oral histories of LGBTI people across South India as a CCDS Open Space Pune fellow, collecting videos from 24 individuals representing marginalised identities. Alongside Rumi Harish, he initiated a study on non-discrimination at Alternative Law Forum (ALF). He co-authored the report ‘Conversations on Caste Discrimination in South India,’ conducted after over 95 conversations throughout the region. Sunil identifies as a Trans Man. In 2018, he co-founded Raahi, an organisation dedicated to the rights of marginalised genders and sexualities. In 2024, he received the Kamala Bhasin Award for his contributions to gender and sexuality rights. About the Moderator: 
 Vijeta Kumar  teaches Communicative English and Creative Writing at St. Joseph’s University, Bengaluru. Limited spots available,  RSVP  at the earliest.

name: Brachio: A Dramatic Interactive Reading for Children by Venkataraghavan Srinivasan
description: Join us  for a dramatic, interactive storytelling session that brings the book  Brachio by Venkataraghavan Srinivasan , vividly to life.The session will close with a hands-on activity that lets you be part of the story! Here are the event details:  Date:  26th April, Sunday  Time:  11 am Venue:  Champaca, Indiranagar Age : 6 years and above Price:  Rs. 599 (This includes a Rs. 299 Champaca Voucher, redeemable at our stores) RSVP  here to block your spot! About the Book: Brachio   is a warm, illustrated children’s book about a very tall, kind-hearted creature who lives by the beach and loves helping others. When the villagers no longer need him, Brachio sets off on a journey, trying to understand his place in the world.  Along the way, through a series of playful and unexpected adventures, he begins to discover that “being tall” isn’t just physical—it’s about purpose, kindness, and belonging. At its heart, the book is about self-discovery, feeling useful, and finding where you truly fit. About the Author: Venkataraghavan is a writer and actor from south India. "Brachio", his illustrated chapter book for early readers, was published by HarperCollins in Dec 2025. Through the protagonist’s extraordinary height, it highlights themes of friendship and self-respect. The book is adapted from an eponymous play for children, also written, directed, produced and performed by Venkataraghavan. In 2024, he was awarded the inaugural year-long Neev Fellowship for Children’s Book Creators. He is at work on a World War Two historical fiction novel for middle-grade readers set in the Madras Zoo. In 2021, Venkataraghavan’s nonfiction book, “The Origin Story of India’s States”, was published by Penguin Random House. His short fiction has appeared in The Iowa Review and The Bombay Literary Magazine, among others, and has been included in anthologies and prize shortlists. Limited slots available,  RSVP  at the earliest!
